Resident Accountant Job at Macden Communications Limited




        
    

        
        Macden Communications Limited is into the supply and distribution of beverages and is a major distributor for International Breweries PLC, Nigerian Breweries Limited, Guiness Nigerian Limited.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Resident Accountant

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time
Working Time and Date: 8am to 6pm (Mondays to Saturdays and Public Holidays)

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Responsible for managing day to day financial transactions
	Maintaining accurate records
	Ensuring Regulatory Compliance
	Preparing Financial Reports
	Book Keeping and General Ledger
	Reconciliation
	Accounts payables and receivables
	Tax Compliance
	Audit Support.


Requirements


	Interested applicants must have a degree in accounting and must have worked within 3-5 years in a warehouse setting and must be knowledgeable in business and computer literate
	Interested applicant must be reside in Rivers State in Port Harcourt Local Govt Area.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: patricia_asemota@yahoo.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: For more inquiries, candidates should call: 09063461304.

title: Job Vacancies at Felton Energy Services Limited
content:
Felton Energy Services Limited (FESL) is an indigenous oil & gas procurement, operation and maintenance company in Nigeria. The company was established to supply global customers with Electrical, Mechanical, Industrial and Engineering Products, on time and at the most competitive prices. FESL has unrivalled commitment to the Oil & Gas Industry and has gained support of the leading manufacturers which ensures our solutions are at the forefront oftoday’s technology.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Instrumentation Specialist

Job Locations: Lagos and Port Harcourt - Rivers

Job Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Instrumentation Specialist will be responsible for ensuring the proper calibration, functionality, and reliability of all measuring instruments used within Felton Energy Services Limited.
	This role is critical to maintaining operational accuracy, compliance with industry standards, and supporting quality assurance across projects.
	The specialist will apply technical expertise and certifications to calibrate, maintain, and troubleshoot instruments, ensuring measurement integrity and efficiency in energy service operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform calibration, verification, and adjustment of measuring instruments in compliance with international standards (ISO/IEC 17025, ANSI/NCSL Z540, etc.).
	Document calibration results, maintain traceability records, and ensure regulatory compliance.
	Conduct preventive maintenance and troubleshooting of instrumentation systems.
	Collaborate with quality assurance teams to uphold industry standards.
	Provide technical support and training to junior staff on calibration procedures.
	Ensure proper documentation of individual competencies and certifications.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Instrumentation, or related field).
	5–7 years of professional experience in instrumentation and calibration services.
	Valid certification/accreditation in calibration of measuring instruments (ISO/IEC 17025 or equivalent).
	Strong knowledge of instrumentation systems, sensors, and measurement technologies.
	Proficiency in calibration software and tools.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and documentation skills.
	Demonstrated ability to interpret and apply technical standards in practical calibration scenarios.


Competency Requirements:


	Documented evidence of individual competencies in calibration and instrumentation.
	Duly certified in the calibration of measuring instruments.
	Strong communication and teamwork skills with a commitment to precision and reliability.


Benefits


	Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
	Professional development opportunities, including certification renewal support.
	Comprehensive health and wellness package.
	Collaborative and innovative work environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: obianuju@feltonenergy.net using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Measurement / Calibration Engineer

Job Locations: Lagos and Port Harcourt - Rivers

Job Purpose


	The Measurement/Calibration Engineer will ensure the accuracy, reliability, and compliance of all measuring instruments and calibration systems used within Felton Energy Services Limited.
	This role is critical to maintaining operational excellence, supporting quality assurance, and meeting industry standards.
	The engineer will be responsible for performing precise calibrations, documenting competencies, and ensuring that all instrumentation aligns with regulatory and client requirements.


Key Responsibilities


	Perform calibration, verification, and adjustment of measuring instruments in compliance with international standards (ISO/IEC 17025, ANSI/NCSL Z540, etc.).
	Document calibration results, maintain traceability records, and ensure regulatory compliance.
	Conduct preventive maintenance and troubleshooting of instrumentation systems.
	Collaborate with quality assurance teams to maintain industry standards.
	Provide technical support and training to junior staff on calibration procedures.
	Ensure proper documentation of individual competencies and certifications.


 Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Instrumentation, or related field).
	5–7 years of professional experience in calibration engineering or instrumentation services.
	Valid certification/accreditation in calibration of measuring instruments (ISO/IEC 17025 or equivalent).
	Strong knowledge of instrumentation systems, sensors, and measurement technologies.
	Proficiency in calibration software and tools.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and documentation skills.
	Demonstrated ability to interpret and apply technical standards in practical calibration scenarios.


Competency Requirements:


	Documented evidence of individual competencies in calibration and instrumentation.
	Duly certified in the calibration of measuring instruments.
	Strong communication and teamwork skills with a commitment to precision and reliability.


 Benefits


	Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
	Professional development opportunities, including certification renewal support.
	Comprehensive health and wellness package.
	Collaborative and innovative work environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: obianuju@feltonenergy.net using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Senior Business Development Manager

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a highly experienced Senior Business Development Manager with a proven track record of leading business analysis activities within the Nigerian energy sector.
	The ideal candidate must have hands-on experience conducting business with NUIMS, NNPC, NUIM Antan, NGC, NEPL, and multiple International Oil Companies (IOCs) on a project basis.
	This role requires deep industry knowledge, exceptional stakeholder management skills, and the ability to translate business needs into actionable project requirements.


Key Responsibilities
Business Analysis & Requirements Management:


	Lead end-to-end business analysis for energy and oil & gas sector projects.
	Gather, validate, and document business and technical requirements.
	Conduct gap analysis, feasibility studies, and impact assessments.
	Create functional specifications, process maps, and business cases.


Stakeholder Engagement:


	Manage high-level stakeholders across NUIMS, NNPC, NUIM Antan, NGC, NEPL, and various IOCs.
	Facilitate workshops, requirement sessions, and steering committee reviews.
	Build and maintain strong collaborative relationships with internal and external partners.


Project Delivery:


	Work closely with project managers, technical teams, and vendor partners to ensure alignment with business goals.
	Support project planning, scope definition, and risk assessment.
	Validate deliverables and drive user acceptance testing (UAT).
	Track benefits realization and ensure project outcomes add business value.


Data & Process Optimization:


	Analyze operational and business data to identify process inefficiencies.
	Recommend improvements to optimize workflows and decision-making.
	Support digital transformation initiatives within the energy/oil & gas domain.


Required Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Engineering, IT, Economics, or related field (Master’s preferred).
	5–10+ years of experience as a Business Analyst, ideally in the energy, oil & gas, or government-regulated industries.
	Demonstrated experience conducting business and managing projects with:
	NUIMS
	NNPC
	NUIM Antan
	NGC
	NEPL
	Major IOCs
	Strong understanding of Nigerian energy-sector operations, compliance, and commercial frameworks.
	Proven experience in requirements gathering, stakeholder management, and process improvement.
	Familiarity with industry standards, project management methodologies, and digital transformation tools.


Key Skills:


	Excellent communication and presentation skills.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities.
	Strategic thinking and business acumen.
	Proficiency with business analysis tools and documentation standards.
	Ability to manage multiple projects and deliver under tight timelines.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: wendy@feltonenergy.net using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Unilag Microfinance Bank has made appreciable investments in technology with a view to being a leading digital bank in Nigeria. With sufficient capital base and experienced team of board members and management, the Bank is positioned to deliver effective and efficient banking services targeted at the young and the middle class without prejudice to the elites.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Location:&nbsp;Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

	Marketing and sales of bank&rsquo;s products

Deposit Mobilization Officer Job at Unilag Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Unilag Microfinance Bank was duly registered in April 2020 with the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) and was licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria to operate as a microfinance bank in February 2021. The focus of the Bank is to provide microfinance banking services to individuals and businesses within the University of Lagos environment as well as small and medium scale enterprises beyond. Unilag Microfinance Bank has made appreciable investments in technology with a view to being a leading digital bank in Nigeria. With sufficient capital base and experienced team of board members and management, the Bank is positioned to deliver effective and efficient banking services targeted at the young and the middle class without prejudice to the elites.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Deposit Mobilization Officer
Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties


	Marketing and sales of bank’s products
	Account opening and customer acquisition
	Persuading clients to save
	Managing new and existing deposit clients
	Developing an implementing a business development plans for identified target markets
	Mobilization and retention of deposit
	Operate in compliance with laws and regulations guiding Microfinance institution.


Qualifications


	Minimum of an OND qualification from any reputable institution.
	Proven working experience as a Deposit Mobilization Officer in a Microfinance Institution is an added advantage.
	20-30 years of age is preferable.
	Good communication & relationship mgt skills.
	Strong marketing and negotiation skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@unilagmfbank.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

  "title": "Fiber Optics Engineer",
  "description": "Cobranet Limited; a reputable organization incorporated in 2003 and provides internet solutions. Our footprint and range of products have grown immensely since incorporation and we are proud to provide every segment of society with a reliable link to the digital universe.

Our entire network topology is rugged, dynamic and fully redundant with negligible downtimes: content showing extremely high availability exceeding 99.5%. Our promise is to continue providing innovative solutions at the best feature-to-price and service-to-price ratio.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fiber Optics Engineer
Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Conduct fiber installations, splicing, and terminations for customer premises and network infrastructure.
	Perform live fiber ring cuts with minimal service disruption.
	Test and certify fiber connections using industry-standard equipment (OTDR, power meters, etc.).
	Ensure proper bonding, grounding, and fiber protection according to technical standards.
	Maintain accurate records of fiber routes, splicing schedules, patch panels, and termination points.
	Support network deployment, acceptance, and commissioning efforts in collaboration with engineering teams.
	Troubleshoot and resolve fiber faults or performance issues in a timely manner.
	Monitor fiber-related alarms and take appropriate corrective actions.
	Maintain tools and ensure proper safety procedures during field operations.
	Provide detailed reports and feedback to technical leadership on field conditions and service quality.


Requirements


	B.Sc or HND in Electrical/Electronic Engineering, Computer Engineering, Telecommunications, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4 years of hands-on experience in fiber optics installation, splicing, testing, and troubleshooting.
	Industry certifications such as FOA Certified Fiber Optic Technician, CCNA, or CompTIA Network+ are an advantage.
	Competence in using tools such as OTDRs, fusion splicers, power meters, and fiber identifiers.
	Strong understanding of Cisco switching and routing configuration and troubleshooting.
	Experience with multi-vendor telecommunications equipment and integration.
	Knowledge of metro fiber, PON, and OSP infrastructure.
	Proficient in maintaining documentation of fiber networks.
	Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work both independently and within a team.
	Flexibility to work outside regular hours including night shifts and weekends as needed.


Additional Requirements:


	Must be a Nigerian citizen.
	Physically fit and able to work at heights, in confined spaces, and in varying environmental conditions.
	Valid driver&rsquo;s license
	Must be proactive, detail-oriented, and possess excellent diagnostic and problem-solving skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@cobranet.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Fiber Optics Engineer Job at Cobranet Limited




        
    

        
        Cobranet Limited; a reputable organization incorporated in 2003 and provides internet solutions. Our footprint and range of products have grown immensely since incorporation and we are proud to provide every segment of society with a reliable link to the digital universe.

Our entire network topology is rugged, dynamic and fully redundant with negligible downtimes: content showing extremely high availability exceeding 99.5%. Our promise is to continue providing innovative solutions at the best feature-to-price and service-to-price ratio.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fiber Optics Engineer
Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Key Responsibilities


	Conduct fiber installations, splicing, and terminations for customer premises and network infrastructure.
	Perform live fiber ring cuts with minimal service disruption.
	Test and certify fiber connections using industry-standard equipment (OTDR, power meters, etc.).
	Ensure proper bonding, grounding, and fiber protection according to technical standards.
	Maintain accurate records of fiber routes, splicing schedules, patch panels, and termination points.
	Support network deployment, acceptance, and commissioning efforts in collaboration with engineering teams.
	Troubleshoot and resolve fiber faults or performance issues in a timely manner.
	Monitor fiber-related alarms and take appropriate corrective actions.
	Maintain tools and ensure proper safety procedures during field operations.
	Provide detailed reports and feedback to technical leadership on field conditions and service quality.


Requirements


	B.Sc or HND in Electrical/Electronic Engineering, Computer Engineering, Telecommunications, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4 years of hands-on experience in fiber optics installation, splicing, testing, and troubleshooting.
	Industry certifications such as FOA Certified Fiber Optic Technician, CCNA, or CompTIA Network+ are an advantage.
	Competence in using tools such as OTDRs, fusion splicers, power meters, and fiber identifiers.
	Strong understanding of Cisco switching and routing configuration and troubleshooting.
	Experience with multi-vendor telecommunications equipment and integration.
	Knowledge of metro fiber, PON, and OSP infrastructure.
	Proficient in maintaining documentation of fiber networks.
	Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work both independently and within a team.
	Flexibility to work outside regular hours including night shifts and weekends as needed.


Additional Requirements:


	Must be a Nigerian citizen.
	Physically fit and able to work at heights, in confined spaces, and in varying environmental conditions.
	Valid driver’s license
	Must be proactive, detail-oriented, and possess excellent diagnostic and problem-solving skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@cobranet.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
